ci_support_linux_64_python3.10.____cpyth:
c_stdlib:
- sysroot
c_stdlib_version:
- '2.17'
channel_sources:
- conda-forge
channel_targets:
- conda-forge main
cxx_compiler:
- gxx
cxx_compiler_version:
- '14'
docker_image:
- quay.io/condaforge/linux-anvil-x86_64:alma9
pin_run_as_build:
  python:
    min_pin: x.x
    max_pin: x.x
python:
- 3.10.* *_cpython
sdl2:
- '2'
sdl2_image:
- '2'
sdl2_mixer:
- '2'
sdl2_ttf:
- '2'
target_platform:
- linux-64

about.description:
Pygame is a free and open-source cross-platform library
for the development of multimedia applications like video games using Python.
It uses the `Simple DirectMedia Layer library` and several other
popular libraries to abstract the most common functions, making writing
these programs a more intuitive task.

This distribution is called **'pygame - Community Edition'** ('pygame-ce' for short).

It is a fork of the upstream pygame project by its former core developers,
and was created after impossible challenges prevented them from continuing development upstream.
The new distribution aims to offer more frequent releases, continuous bugfixes and enhancements,
and a more democratic governance model.

name: pygame-ce

about.description:
Pygame is a free and open-source cross-platform library
for the development of multimedia applications like video games using Python.
It uses the `Simple DirectMedia Layer library` and several other
popular libraries to abstract the most common functions, making writing
these programs a more intuitive task.

This distribution is called **'pygame - Community Edition'** ('pygame-ce' for short).

It is a fork of the upstream pygame project by its former core developers,
and was created after impossible challenges prevented them from continuing development upstream.
The new distribution aims to offer more frequent releases, continuous bugfixes and enhancements,
and a more democratic governance model.

Pygame is a free and open-source cross-platform library
for the development of multimedia applications like video games using Python.
It uses the `Simple DirectMedia Layer library` and several other
popular libraries to abstract the most common functions, making writing
these programs a more intuitive task.

This distribution is called **'pygame - Community Edition'** ('pygame-ce' for short).

It is a fork of the upstream pygame project by its former core developers,

raw_meta_yaml:
context:
  version: 2.5.7

package:
  name: pygame-ce
  version: ${{ version }}

source:
  - url: https://pypi.org/packages/source/p/pygame-ce/pygame_ce-${{ version }}.tar.gz
    sha256: 86beb797cd73c141299a29b56f7df2b0543fbdc81d428022458329ff694aaa51
    patches:
      - patches/meson-search-conda-prefix.patch

build:
  script: ${{ PYTHON }} -m pip install .
  number: 1

requirements:
  build:
    - if: build_platform != host_platform
      then:
        - python
        - cross-python_${{ host_platform }}
        - cython
    - ${{ compiler('cxx') }}
    - ${{ stdlib('c') }}
    - cmake
    - pkg-config
  host:
    - python
    - meson-python
    - meson
    - ninja
    - cython
    - sphinx
    - astroid
    - sphinx-autoapi
    - pyproject-metadata
    - pip
    - portmidi
    - sdl2
    - sdl2_mixer
    - sdl2_image
    - sdl2_ttf
    - libfreetype
    - if: linux
      then: xorg-xorgproto
  run:
    - python
  run_constraints:
    - pygame <0

tests:
  - python:
      imports:
        - pygame
      pip_check: true

about:
  summary: Python Game Development
  description: |
    Pygame is a free and open-source cross-platform library
    for the development of multimedia applications like video games using Python.
    It uses the `Simple DirectMedia Layer library` and several other
    popular libraries to abstract the most common functions, making writing
    these programs a more intuitive task.

    This distribution is called **'pygame - Community Edition'** ('pygame-ce' for short).

    It is a fork of the upstream pygame project by its former core developers,
    and was created after impossible challenges prevented them from continuing development upstream.
    The new distribution aims to offer more frequent releases, continuous bugfixes and enhancements,
    and a more democratic governance model.
  license: LGPL-2.1-or-later
  license_file: docs/LGPL.txt
  documentation: https://pyga.me/docs
  homepage: https://pyga.me/

extra:
  recipe-maintainers:
    - h-vetinari
    - kcemenike
    - Tobias-Fischer
    - traversaro
